The
Gentle
Touch.

Advocating for children with eczema

We are a 501(c)(3) non-profit organization dedicated to advocating and helping children who are suffering from eczema. We believe every child deserves to feel comfortable in their own skin — and we're here to make that possible.

Support Our Mission Learn About Eczema

"Every child deserves to feel comfortable in their own skin. Together, we raise awareness, provide resources, and build community for families navigating eczema."

Our Story

Meet the Founders

Co-Founder & Director

Dhriti Rao

Dhriti has lived with eczema her entire life, experiencing firsthand the challenges — the discomfort, the self-consciousness, and the difficulty finding resources. Her personal journey inspired her to create a space where children and families never have to feel alone in their struggle. She founded The Gentle Touch to turn her experience into advocacy and real, meaningful change.

✨ Eczema Advocate 🌊 Beach Lover 💜 Community Builder
Co-Founder & Director

Isha Kale

Isha has eczema herself and knows firsthand the challenges that come with growing up with sensitive skin. Her love for working with kids and her dream of becoming a veterinarian reflect her deep compassion for those who can't always speak for themselves — whether children or animals. She co-founded The Gentle Touch because she believes every child deserves to feel seen, supported, and comfortable in their own skin.

🌸 Eczema Advocate 🐾 Future Veterinarian 🧡 Loves Working with Kids
501(c)(3) Certified Non-Profit · All donations are tax-deductible · Founded by Dhriti Rao & Isha Kale

Understanding Eczema

What Is
Eczema?

Eczema (atopic dermatitis) is a chronic inflammatory skin condition that causes the skin to become itchy, red, dry, and cracked. It is not contagious — it is caused by a combination of genetic and environmental factors that affect the skin's ability to retain moisture.

For children, eczema can be especially difficult. Beyond the physical discomfort, kids often face social and emotional challenges — feeling self-conscious, struggling to sleep, and missing out on activities due to flare-ups.

There is currently no cure, but with the right resources, support, and awareness, children and families can manage eczema and thrive.

31M+
Americans affected by eczema
1 in 5
Children develop eczema before age 5
60%
Report significant impact on quality of life
🔬

Causes & Triggers

Eczema is linked to a deficiency in the skin barrier, causing it to lose moisture and allow irritants in. Common triggers include dry weather, soaps, detergents, stress, sweat, and certain fabrics. Food allergies may also play a role in some children.

💊

Symptoms to Know

Watch for intense itching (often worse at night), dry and sensitive skin, red-to-brownish-gray patches, small raised bumps, thickened or cracked skin, and raw, swollen skin from scratching. In children, rashes often appear on the face, scalp, and extremities.

🌿

Management & Care

While there's no cure, eczema can be managed with consistent moisturizing, identifying and avoiding triggers, topical treatments prescribed by a dermatologist, and gentle skincare routines. Early intervention makes a huge difference for children.

Why Children Need Our Advocacy

Children with eczema often can't fully communicate their discomfort, leading to sleep deprivation, irritability, and difficulty concentrating at school. Parents frequently feel overwhelmed navigating treatments, school accommodations, and their child's emotional wellbeing.

The Gentle Touch exists to bridge this gap — connecting families with resources, raising awareness in communities and schools, and advocating for better support systems for children living with eczema.

😴 Sleep disruption from nighttime itching
🏫 School challenges and social anxiety
👨‍👩‍👧 Family burden and caregiver stress
💜 Emotional impact on self-esteem
🩺 Access to care and dermatology resources
